_comment: PII-redaction eval corpus (JAM-277). Each case carries `entities`: spans labelled is_pii=true (real personal data, drives recall) or is_pii=false (hard negatives — orgs/products/public reference data that must NOT be masked). Any mask that doesn't overlap a true-PII span is counted a false positive, so negatives need not be exhaustively labelled. `source`: 'seeded' = excerpt of a real drafting-eval scenario (data/qa/complaint_scenarios.json); 'synthetic' = purpose-built.

cases.text: I am writing to complain. My name is Mr John Smith and you can reach me at john.smith@example.com about the mishandling of my account.
Thierry Henry here — I have been trying to resolve this for weeks. Please call me on 07911 123456 as the branch never responds.
An unauthorised charge appeared on my card 4242 4242 4242 4242, and the refund was sent to the wrong sort code 12-34-56.
My account number 12345678 was debited in error. I live at 14 Mornington Crescent, London NW1 7RD and have had no reply.
For reference my National Insurance number is AB123456C. I was told my complaint about the pension transfer would be escalated.
Your final response told me to contact the Financial Ombudsman Service at Exchange Tower, London E14 9SR, telephone 0800 023 4567, but you never enclosed the leaflet.
I am disputing a fraudulent charge to my card for Tottenham Hotspur tickets that I never purchased.
As Mrs Patel explained, your letter cited DISP 1.6.2R and DISP 2.8.2R but still failed to mention the eight-week deadline.
Both Mr David Jones and his wife Susan Jones were affected; please email confirmation to david.jones@mail.co.uk.
The agent read out a reference 1234 5678 9012 3456 which is not my card, yet still charged my real account number 87654321.
They quoted DA123456C as my NI number which is wrong; please call me on 020 7946 0958 instead.
I switched to your Digital Flex Saver after the Universal Credit payment bounced, and then Card Protection Plan charges appeared.
Dr Aisha Khan of 22 Baker Street, London W1U 6RX reports the standing order to sort code 09-01-29 failed twice.
Charge disputed on Mastercard 5555 5555 5555 4444; please send the outcome to a.patel@example.org.
the customer thierry henry says we ignored him and never replied to his emails.
My complaint about Westfield Banking PLC was handled by Mr Tom Hardy in your Leeds office.

I made a complaint to my bank, Westfield Banking PLC, six weeks ago about a payment protection insurance policy. Three weeks after I complained, the bank wrote to me saying they were forwarding my complaint to the insurer.
During the application process, I noticed that a 'Card Protection Plan' at £7.99/month was already pre-selected as part of the account setup. I only found out about the Ombudsman from a friend.
I took out a peer-to-peer loan of £15,000 through LendConnect Platform in March this year. Within 10 days I sent an email to the address specified in the contract stating I wished to withdraw.
On top of that, my payments are being applied to these charges first rather than reducing the shortfall. I am on Universal Credit and struggling to keep up as it is.

I opened an execution-only share dealing account with your firm last year to buy and sell shares listed on the London Stock Exchange. When I signed up, I was never told anything about your complaints procedure.
I used a price comparison website called QuickLoans Compare to find a short-term loan. A more expensive lender called FastCash was displayed at the top with a prominent 'Recommended' badge.
